question.text: Which of the following is NOT one of the five main factors that affect the price (premium) of an option?

answer.text: The trading volume of the option contract
The spot price of the underlying asset
The volatility of the underlying asset

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** The 5 factors affecting option price are: 1. Spot price (S) 2. Strike price (X) 3. Volatility (σ) 4. Time to expiry (T) 5. Risk-free interest rate (r)
Trading volume affects liquidity but is NOT a direct input to option pricing models.
**The Trap:** While higher volume may tighten bid-ask spreads, it is not a factor in theoretical option pricing models like Black-Scholes.
**Cross-Exam Context:** These 5 inputs are the basis of the Black-Scholes model.
**Key Takeaway:** 5 factors: S, X, σ, T, r. Remember "SXVTR" - Spot, Strike, Volatility, Time, Rate.
